content.question: Would removing early optional tracks in Hungary produce distributional gains (reduced inequality) comparable to Poland's post-1999 gains, or are the structural and political-economy differences too great for valid comparison?
content.reduction_path: Analyze whether Hungarian non-selected cohorts show opportunity or attainment gaps similar to Poland's pre-1999 vocational-track populations. Examine whether Hungary's majority comprehensive-schooling population shows patterns of disadvantage relative to track-selected students that match Poland's documented inequality reductions. This requires longitudinal student outcome data by track exposure and socio-economic origin.
